title: Fire and Rescue Force (Safety Inspections and Certificates) Regulations, 2008
title_en: Fire and Rescue Force (Safety Inspections and Certificates) Regulations, 2008

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Fire and Rescue Force (Safety Inspections and Certificates) Regulations, 2008.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: “ Fire Authority ” means the Commissioner-General of the Force and includes fire and rescue officers authorized by him generally or specifically to exercise powers, functions and duties conferred by the Act; “ The Commissioner-General ” means the Commissioner-General of the Force appointed under Section 14(1) of the Act; “ Fire Safety Certifiicate ” means the certificate or sticker issued by the appropriate Fire Authority which permits the use of a premise, vehicles, vessels and any other conveyance facility after the fire safety inspection.

provisions.title: Inspection of premises
provisions.content: (1) for the purposes of these Regulations, inspection shall be carried out and Fire Safety Certificate shall be issued for premises of the following categories: (a) hotels, guest houses, cottages, hostels and any other business facilities of accommodation or sleeping capable of occupying more than six persons; (b) institutions providing hospitalization, treatment or care of persons; (c) facilities for entertainment, recreation or instruction or clubs, society or association; (d) premises of educational institutions and other institutions of learning, training or research; (e) premises of institutions whose access is open to the public or members only whether on payment or otherwise; (f) premises used as a place of work where more than ten persons in total are at work in the whole of the premises at any one time; (g) premises or facility used as a place for storage of flammable liquids, gas or chemicals; (h) gas pipelines; (i) gas plant; (j) mines; (k) power generating facilities; (l) facilities used or gain storage and milling; (m) facilities for storage of explosives; (n) any other facility which in the opinion of the Commissioner General is a fire hazard. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , for purposes of vehicles, vessels and any other conveyance facility, inspection shall be carried out and Fire Safety Certificate shall be issued and displayed on the windscreen of that particular facility of any other conspicuous place as the Commissioner-General may approve. (3) There shall be an inspection fee for each category of premises and facilities to be inspected as shall be determined by the Minister.

provisions.title: Owner of premises to render information
provisions.content: Subject to regulation 3(1) and (2) , the owner of premises, vehicle, vessels or any other conveyance facility shall render full information in respect of fire precaution to the Fire Authority during inspection.

provisions.title: Application to be made to the Fire Authority
provisions.content: (1) Any person who is an owner or operator of a premises, vehicle, vessels or any other conveyance facility which has not been inspected and issued with a Fire Safety Certificate by the Fire Authority shall apply in writing to the Fire Authority for the conduct of inspection in his premises, vehicle, vessels or any other conveyance facility. (2) Any person who contravenes sub regulation (1) commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not exceeding one million shillings or imprisonment for a term of not exceeding twelve months or to both.

provisions.title: Owner's of premises to observe safety and train staff
provisions.content: (1) While the application for a Fire Safety Certificate is pending, the owner or operator of a premise, vehicle, vessel and any other conveyance facility shall ensure that: (a) the means of escape in case of fire at the premises, vehicle, vessels or any other conveyance facility is safe and can effectively used at all times when there are people on the premises, vehicle, vessel or any other conveyance facility; (b) the means for fighting fire with which the premise, vehicle, vessel and any other conveyance facility are provided are maintained in efficient order; (c) any person employed to work in the premise, vehicle, vessel and any other conveyance facility receives instructions or training on fire precaution and management through a program approved by the Commissioner-General. (2) Where the premises, vehicle, vessels or any other conveyance facility is located at any location where there is no Fire Authority , the owner or operator of the premises, vehicle, vessel or any other conveyance facility in question shall incur the full cost of the conduct of the inspection. (3) In the case of passenger service vehicles, the owner shall be required to observe that— (a) all windows are not obstructed in any way at all times when the vehicle is in use; (b) the fire fighting facilities installed in the vehicle are maintained and kept in serviceable condition at all times; (c) any person employed to work in receives instructions or training in what do in case of emergency; and (d) the vehicle does not carry hazardous materials set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations or any other materials in any manner likely to explode or otherwise cause fire in the vehicle while in use.

provisions.title: Exemption Fire Safety Certificate
provisions.content: The Fire Authority shall inspect and consider whether the premises qualify for exemption from the requirements to have a Fire Safety Certificate.

provisions.title: Informa­tion
provisions.content: ( 1) Before a Fire Safety Certificate is granted, the Fire Authority may require the owner or operator to provide information relating to the premises, vehicle, vessel or conveyance facility. (2) Plans should comprise of drawings showing the essential features, although a Fire Authority may accept architects plan if they are available and suitable for the purpose. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (1), plans of the premises or vessel to be inspected may be required by the Fire Authority and if such plans are not provided within the specified time or such further time as the Fire Authority may allow, the owner or operator of the premises vehicle, vessel or any other conveyance facility commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not exceeding two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not exceeding three months or to both. (4) Where the owner or operator is convicted for a second offence under sub-regulation (3) , he shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not exceeding six months or both, and where the owner or operator is convicted for a third and subsequent offence under sub-regulation (3) he shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not exceeding one million shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months or to both.

provisions.title: Content of a Precau­tion plan
provisions.content: The fire precaution plan in a premise, vehicle, vessel or conveyance facility shall specify— (a) the use of the premises, vehicle, vessel or conveyance facility; (b) the means of escape in case of fire; (c) the methods for ensuring that the means of escape are safely and effectively used at all material times which will cover such matters as measures to restrict the spread of fire, smoke and fumes, escape lighting, direction sign and assembly points; (d) the types, number and location of the fire alarms and equipment; (e) particulars of any explosive or highly flammable materials which may be stored or used in the premises; and (f) arrangements for periodic fire drills for staff and other occupants.

(1) Where on inspection the Fire Authority is not satisfied with what is provided for under paragraph (b) and (c) of regulation 9 , it shall serve a notice on the owner or operator stating the defects and steps to be taken by the owner or operator within a specified time to correct the defects before issuance of a Fire Safety Certificate. (2) Subject to any specified time that may be given to correct a defect identified during inspection, a Fire Safety Certificate shall not be issued by the fire Authority to any owner or operator of a premises, vehicle, vessel or conveyance facility which is found to be a fire hazard it shall be unlawful to use the premises, vehicle, vessel or conveyance facility without a Fire Safety Certificate.

provisions.title: Power of Fire Authority
provisions.content: The Fire Authority may, during the inspection, take into account any other requirement not covered by these Regulations but necessary for purposes of fire precaution before issuance of the Fire Safety Certificate.

provisions.title: Validity of certificate
provisions.content: The Fire Safety Certificate shall remain valid for one year.

provisions.title: Information on explosives and highly flammable materials
provisions.content: Every owner of the factory or dealer of explosives or highly flammable materials shall inform the Commissioner-General before storing or using explosives or highly flammable materials in his premises.

(1) The fees set out in the Second Schedule to these Regulations shall be paid to the Commissioner-General in each financial year. (2) Any person who fails to pay the fees prescribed under the Second Schedule to these Regulations commits an offence and on conviction shall be liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or to both.

provisions.title: Forms
provisions.content: The Commissioner-General shall issue standard forms for use in the implementation of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes the provision of these Regulations where no specific penalty is prescribed commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine of not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to an imprisonment for a term of exceeding three months. definitions.definition: the Commissioner-General of the Force and includes fire and rescue officers authorized by him generally or specifically to exercise powers, functions and duties conferred by the Act
the Commissioner-General of the Force appointed under Section 14(1) of the Act
the certificate or sticker issued by the appropriate Fire Authority which permits the use of a premise, vehicles, vessels and any other conveyance facility after the fire safety inspection.
